step_rationale: Antioxidant reducing thrombosis; predicts adverse events and thrombus burden in atherosclerotic CAD/STEMI.
mechanism: Bilirubin exerts antioxidant and antithrombotic effects, with low levels predicting thrombus burden and adverse outcomes in Type 1 MI.

references.title: Multi-omics data integration from patients with carotid stenosis illuminates key molecular signatures of atherosclerotic instability.
Genotype-based "virtual" metabolomics in a clinical biobank identifies novel metabolite-disease associations.
Genotype-based "virtual" metabolomics in a clinical biobank identifies novel metabolite-disease associations.
Mildly elevated unconjugated bilirubin is associated with reduced platelet activation-related thrombogenesis and inflammation in Gilbert's syndrome.
Bilirubin Levels and Thrombus Burden in Patients With ST-Segment Elevation Myocardial Infarction.

trials.title: Validation of Novel Metabolic, Inflammatory, and Hepatic-Renal Biomarkers-HbA1c/C-peptide Ratio, Neutrophil/HDL Ratio, and ALBI Score-for Predicting the No-Reflow Phenomenon After Primary PCI in Patients With STEMI.
interventions: HbA1c/C-peptide ratio Albumin-bilirubin (ALBI) score. Neutrophil/HDL ratio
